entries.definition:
ṣ-kh-kh deafening sound, a cry that deafens by its vehemence; to pierce; calamity. Of this root, one form, صاخّة ṣākhkhatun, occurs once in the Qurʾan.
صاخّة ṣākhkhatun [act. part./n.] that which deafens by its vehemence; the cry on the occasion of which the Resurrection will take place, the calamity of the Resurrection (80:33–4) فَإِذَا جَاءَتِ الصَّاخَّةُ يَوْمَ يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ when the Deafening Blast comes—the Day when one will flee from one’s own brother.

صخر ṣakhr (no verbal root) [generic noun occurring once] rock, rocks (89:9) وَثَمُودَ الَّذِينَ جَابُوا الصَّخْرَ بِالْوَادِ and [with the tribe of] Thamūd,

514
